$comment: PacketPilot — New Universal Login theme. Styles the login BOX (colors, borders, fonts, logo). The split-screen surround is in page-template.liquid. Apply by merging these values into your tenant's theme: GET /api/v2/branding/themes/default, overlay this, then PATCH /api/v2/branding/themes/{themeId} (or POST to create one). The theme API validates the FULL object, so start from your live theme rather than sending only these keys. See README.md.

fonts.$comment: font_url must point to a font FILE (woff/woff2 with CORS), not a stylesheet. Auth0 documents WOFF/WOFF2 but does NOT document variable-font support — this is the Geist variable woff2 (one file, all weights); TEST it in a non-prod tenant first and if the widget font doesn't apply, fall back to a static single-weight woff2 (e.g. https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/@fontsource/geist-sans/files/geist-sans-latin-500-normal.woff2). Air-gapped build: self-host and swap the URL.

page_background.$comment: The page-template.liquid draws the real background/split-screen; this is the fallback the default page would use and the layout hint (widget on the left).

widget.$comment: logo_position:none — the crisp inline radar mark is drawn by the page template above the box, so no hosted logo asset is needed. To use Auth0's own logo slot instead, set logo_position:center and logo_url to a hosted PNG/SVG. NOTE: social_buttons_layout ('top'/'bottom') sets the LABEL position on each social button, NOT whether the social block sits above/below the form — Auth0 controls that placement (it renders below the credentials form by default, matching the reference).
